windows.transcript: candles. Just put your chunks of wax in your melting canister, boil the water in your pot and let the wax melt. This takes a while but fills your home
windows.old_description: Melting amber wax in the pot (double boiler).
windows.embed_text_v2: Melt wax chunks using stovetop double boiler: aluminum pouring pitcher with handle sits in a pot of boiling water while cream-colored wax slowly liquefies into amber liquid.
windows.summary_v2: Static double-boiler shot of solid wax chunks slowly melting into amber liquid inside an aluminum pouring pitcher set in a pot of boiling water on the stovetop.

seconds_v2.desc: Aluminum pouring pitcher with handle sits in a pot of boiling water on a stove; cream-colored wax chunks are partly melted into amber liquid inside the pitcher.
Same double-boiler setup; chunks of wax continue to soften and float in the warmed amber wax pool.
Static shot of the pitcher in the simmering water bath; visible bubbling around the base of the pitcher.
Wax chunks shrink slightly as the bath continues; pitcher and pot unchanged on the burner.
Boiling water rises higher around the pitcher; remaining wax solids float on top of the melting pool.
Continued melt with steady boil; chunks of unmelted wax still visible at one side of the pitcher.
Stable double-boiler frame; the surface of the melted wax is mostly liquid with a few small solid pieces.
Pitcher remains in the rolling boil; wax is nearly fully liquefied.
End of the static melt shot: aluminum pitcher in the boiling pot, wax now almost completely melted to clear amber liquid.

windows.transcript: home with one of the best smells in the world. If you're using real beeswax. If you use old candles, there will sometimes be wicks in there that you
windows.embed_text_v2: melting and stirring amber beeswax in an aluminum pot inside a boiling-water double boiler on a stove, wooden stir stick, pale unmelted wax chunks dissolving, glass measuring jug nearby
windows.summary_v2: A maker melts and stirs amber beeswax chunks in an aluminum pot set inside a boiling-water double boiler on a stove, blending the last solids with a wooden stick.

seconds_v2.desc: Close top-down on an aluminum pouring pot sitting in a larger pot of simmering water (double boiler) on a white stove; the pot holds amber molten beeswax with several pale unmelted chunks floating on the surface.
Same double-boiler setup; the beeswax is mostly liquid amber with a few solid creamy lumps still floating, water bubbling visibly in the outer pot around the inner pot.
Camera holds on the melting beeswax; the unmelted chunks have shrunk slightly as the amber liquid swirls, steam and boiling water around the inner aluminum pot.
View pulls slightly; molten amber beeswax with a couple of soft pale chunks near the pour spout, double boiler still rolling-boiling underneath.
Motion-blurred transition as the shot tilts up; a clear glass measuring jug and a wooden stir stick begin to overlap the frame over the beeswax pot.
A long wooden stir stick now dips into the aluminum pot of amber beeswax; the white Frigidaire stove backsplash and a glass jug are visible behind.
The wooden stick stirs the molten amber beeswax in the aluminum pot, blending in the last pale unmelted bits; double boiler bubbling below.
Continued stirring with the wooden stick through the liquid amber wax; a pale chunk of beeswax still visible dissolving near the spout.
Wooden stick angled into the pot, swirling the smooth amber beeswax as it finishes melting in the simmering-water double boiler.

windows.embed_text_v2: finished beeswax pillar candle burning with a warm flame in a dark room, domed fluted top, cream-yellow wax on a metal plate, glowing reveal shot
windows.summary_v2: A single finished beeswax pillar candle burns steadily in a dark room, its warm flame lighting the domed, fluted top.

seconds_v2.desc: Extreme close-up of a single lit beeswax pillar candle in a dark room; warm yellow flame burns atop a domed, fluted-rim candle on a metal plate, deep black background.
The beeswax candle continues to burn steadily, flame upright and bright, illuminating the glossy domed top and scalloped edge of the candle.
Same finished candle glowing in darkness; the flame leans slightly, casting warm light on the cream-yellow wax body.
Flame flickers taller; molten pool faintly visible at the candle's domed top, dark surroundings unchanged.
Steady burn of the lit beeswax pillar; soft reflection of the flame on the metal dish beneath the candle.
The candle flame sways gently; warm amber glow on the fluted candle and a hint of the dim background plate.
Continued slow-burning beeswax candle, flame bright and centered on the domed top in the dark room.
Flame narrows briefly; the cream-yellow beeswax candle glows warmly against the black backdrop.
Final hold on the single lit beeswax pillar candle burning quietly in darkness, warm flame and softly lit domed top.
